message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent of all others. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correctly addresses the issue, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it, with a discriminating regression test where a code change is involved).

Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix F-008 (fix) in src/assay_engine/pipeline.py
What was done: versioner.put() failure is wrapped as IngestionError (was raw PermissionError).
Regression test(s): test_versioner_failure_is_wrapped_as_ingestion_error

Steps:
1. Read the actual changed file(s) at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
2. Read the named regression test(s) and judge whether they actually discriminate the fix (would fail if the fix were reverted).
3. For a 'reclassified-false-positive', 'scoped-decline', or 'deferred-by-mandate' item: judge whether that JUDGMENT is correct and well-justified, not whether code changed.
4. Decide: appropriate? complete? Overall verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested).

Be skeptical and specific. Return your verdict.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: The fix looks well-implemented. Let me examine the regression test.

content.content:
461	def test_versioner_failure_is_wrapped_as_ingestion_error(tmp_path):
462	    # #F-008: a storage failure in the optional versioner seam must surface as the documented
463	    # IngestionError, not a raw untyped exception the caller cannot distinguish from a firewall
464	    # breach. Pre-fix: versioner.put() was called bare and PermissionError propagated raw.
465	    from assay_engine.pipeline import IngestionError
466	
467	    class BoomVersioner:
468	        def put(self, ref_str):
469	            raise PermissionError("read-only store")
470	
471	        def path_for(self, version):  # pragma: no cover - not reached
472	            raise AssertionError
473	
474	    with pytest.raises(IngestionError, match="data versioner failed"):
475	        _run(tmp_path, DISCOVERY, versioner=BoomVersioner())
476	
477	
478	def test_confirm_held_out_returning_none_raises_firewall_violation(tmp_path):
479	    # #F-021: a confirmer that forgets to `return` yields None; the runner must raise a typed
480	    # FirewallViolation, not die on an opaque AttributeError at verdict.hypothesis_id.
481	    plan = ref.make_plan(ref.write_source(tmp_path / "c.json"), modes=DISCOVERY)
482	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="expected a Verdict"):
483	        run_study(replace(plan, confirm_held_out=lambda h, c: None), gate_handler=auto_approve)
484	
485	
486	def test_empty_discover_in_pipeline_raises(tmp_path):
487	    # #F-042: a discover() returning no hypotheses is a vacuous run; the pipeline must fail loud
488	    # rather than complete with zero verdicts (indistinguishable from a broken callable).
489	    plan = ref.make_plan(ref.write_source(tmp_path / "c.json"), modes=DISCOVERY)
490	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="no corpus units|no hypotheses|discover"):
491	        run_study(replace(plan, discover=lambda c: []), gate_handler=auto_approve)
492	
493	
494	def test_tracker_start_run_failure_warns_and_continues(tmp_path, caplog):
495	    # #F-028: if start_run fails, run_id stays None and ALL metrics silently no-op. The engine
496	    # must emit a structured warning (and a trail entry) so an operator seeing no MLflow run
497	    # knows why, instead of guessing. The run itself still completes.
498	    import logging
499	
500	    class StartBoomTracker:
501	        def start_run(self, name, params):
502	            raise RuntimeError("tracker unreachable")
503	
504	        def log_metric(self, *a):  # pragma: no cover - never reached (run_id is None)
505	            raise AssertionError

content.text: The test is discriminating. Let me verify it passes now and check the `_run` helper and `match` string alignment, then confirm it would fail if reverted.

37	def _run(tmp_path, modes, **kw) -> StudyResult:
38	    src = ref.write_source(tmp_path / "corpus.json")
39	    kw.setdefault("gate_handler", auto_approve)  # gate_handler is required; default to opt-out
40	    return run_study(ref.make_plan(src, modes=modes), **kw)
41	
42	
43	# ---- the workflow composes, both modes ----
44	
45	
46	def test_discovery_only_runs_end_to_end(tmp_path):
47	    res = _run(tmp_path, DISCOVERY)
48	    assert res.phases == required_phases(DISCOVERY)

content.text: Test passes. Now let me confirm it discriminates by simulating the revert (call `versioner.put` bare and check the test would fail).

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && .venv/bin/python -c "
from assay_engine.pipeline import IngestionError
# Confirm IngestionError is ValueError subclass, NOT a parent of PermissionError
print('IngestionError is ValueError:', issubclass(IngestionError, ValueError))
print('PermissionError is IngestionError:', issubclass(PermissionError, IngestionError))
"

content.content:
IngestionError is ValueError: True
PermissionError is IngestionError: False

content.text:
Confirmed discrimination. Pre-fix, `versioner.put()` raised a raw `PermissionError`, which is an `OSError` subclass — not an `IngestionError`/`ValueError`. So `pytest.raises(IngestionError, ...)` would fail on revert. The `match="data versioner failed"` string also matches the wrap message exactly.

The fix uses a broad `except Exception` which correctly normalizes any storage failure (PermissionError, OSError, etc.) into the documented `IngestionError`, with `from exc` preserving the cause. The placement is correct (versioning is part of ingestion). The comment cites the right finding (#F-008).

input.reasoning: Fix at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:406-417 wraps versioner.put() in try/except, re-raising any storage failure as the documented IngestionError with `from exc` (preserves cause). Placement is correct (versioning is part of ingesting the source) and idiomatic; the broad `except Exception` is appropriately scoped to an optional storage seam and noqa-annotated. No regression: only the versioner-failure path is affected; success path unchanged. Regression test test_versioner_failure_is_wrapped_as_ingestion_error (tests/test_pipeline.py:461) is discriminating: it injects a BoomVersioner whose put() raises PermissionError and asserts pytest.raises(IngestionError, match="data versioner failed"). Verified IngestionError is a ValueError subclass and PermissionError is NOT an IngestionError subclass, so pre-fix (raw PermissionError propagating) the test would fail to match IngestionError. Test passes in the venv (1 passed). match string aligns exactly with the wrap message. Both appropriate and complete.

content.text: Verdict: CONFIRMED. The F-008 fix correctly wraps versioner storage failures as the documented `IngestionError` (with cause chaining), and the named regression test genuinely discriminates the fix — it would fail on revert because raw `PermissionError` is not an `IngestionError`/`ValueError` subclass.
